title: How to capitalize only the first letter (sentence case)
task: Make text start with one capital letter without capitalizing every word.

excel.note: PROPER capitalizes EVERY word — this does just the first character.

explanation: Take the first character uppercased, then everything from position 2 lowercased, and join them: "hELLO world" becomes "Hello world". This is what people usually want when they reach for PROPER and get "Hello World" instead. Drop the LOWER wrapper if the rest of the text should keep its existing casing (acronyms, names): =UPPER(LEFT(A2,1))&MID(A2,2,LEN(A2)).
